answer:
\boxed{H}

Michael Van den Haag was a prominent **retentionist**—a proponent of capital punishment—known for his arguments in favor of the death penalty as a just and necessary response to certain crimes. His views were rooted in a conservative, law-and-order perspective rather than any of the other ideological labels provided. His work often clashed with abolitionist positions, reinforcing his stance as a retentionist.
